title: IMD Weather Update: Red alert for Odisha, very heavy rain in Bihar, Marathwada; heatwave to persist in South India - Full details - ET Now

IMD Weather Update: Red alert for Odisha, very heavy rain in Bihar, Marathwada; heatwave to persist in South India - Full details
IMD has forecast very heavy rainfall across Odisha, Bihar, Marathwada and West Bengal, while heatwave and hot, humid conditions are expected to persist in parts of southern and northwestern India.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast widespread heavy to very heavy rainfall across several parts of the country over the next few days, while heatwave and hot, humid conditions are expected to continue in parts of southern and northwestern India.
According to the weather department, Marathwada is likely to receive very heavy rainfall on Wednesday and Thursday, while Bihar is expected to witness very heavy rain until Wednesday.
The IMD has also predicted very heavy rainfall in Gangetic West Bengal and Odisha on Thursday. In the northeastern region, Sub-Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim are likely to experience very heavy showers on Sunday and Monday.
Meanwhile, the weather agency has issued a red alert for parts of Odisha, warning of extremely heavy rainfall through Wednesday. Residents in the affected areas have been advised to remain alert and follow instructions issued by local authorities.
On the other hand, heatwave conditions are expected to continue over isolated parts of Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu and Puducherry until Wednesday.
The IMD has also forecast hot and humid weather across parts of southern peninsular India until Friday, while similar conditions are likely to prevail over parts of northwest India until Thursday. Authorities have advised people to stay hydrated, avoid prolonged exposure to direct sunlight during peak afternoon hours, and monitor official weather updates.
